source_facts: [1] Château Pavie is a classified Bordeaux estate in Saint-Émilion.

question_text: A winemaker is trying to protect fruit quality before harvest after noticing a surge of sticky-sap insects on the vines. The vineyard team also reports heavy ant activity in blocks planted with most cover crops, while one block seeded to vetch shows fewer ants moving into the canopy. The grower is considering a last-minute treatment, but wants to avoid any harvest-date conflict and also restore natural predator activity against the sap-feeding pests. Which plan best fits these goals?
options: [{'id': 'A', 'text': 'Focus only on the honeydew-producing insects, since ants do not affect predator or parasite activity once those insects are established.'}, {'id': 'B', 'text': 'Choose a product only if its required days between application and picking fit the planned harvest window, and keep or expand the vetch cover because it can reduce ants moving into the vines, helping limit their protection of honeydew-producing pests.'}, {'id': 'C', 'text': 'Spray immediately without checking the label timing, and replace the vetch with another cover crop so ants have less nectar available.'}, {'id': 'D', 'text': 'Delay any treatment until after harvest, and increase non-vetch cover crops because more cover vegetation generally reduces ant problems in the fruiting zone.'}]
source_facts:
[1] As part of this relationship, they also protect these honeydew-producing insects from predators and parasites, thus disrupting biological control.
---
[2] Preharvest interval (PHI) is the number of days from treatment to harvest.
---
[3] Management Ants may be more of a problem in vineyards with cover crops; the exception appears to be a vetch cover crop, because it supplies ants with adequate amounts of nectar and keeps them from moving into vines.

question_text: One of these AVAs allows Catawba, while the other instead authorizes Malbec. Which AVA is the one associated with Catawba rather than Malbec?
options: [{'id': 'A', 'text': 'Both AVAs authorize Catawba'}, {'id': 'B', 'text': 'Clear Lake AVA'}, {'id': 'C', 'text': 'Nine Lakes of East Tennessee AVA'}, {'id': 'D', 'text': 'Neither AVA authorizes Catawba'}]
source_facts:
[1] Nine Lakes of East Tennessee AVA permits the Catawba grape variety.
---
[2] Clear Lake AVA permits the Malbec grape variety.

question_text: The fact assigns the Benmore Valley AVA to which US state?
source_facts: [1] The Benmore Valley AVA was established in 1991 in California.

question_text: You are choosing between two wines and want the one with the slightly higher maximum stated alcohol level. One reaches up to 12.0% ABV, while the other tops out at 12%. Which wine has the higher upper-end alcohol figure?
options: [{'id': 'A', 'text': 'Barakoni wine'}, {'id': 'B', 'text': 'Neither has a stated alcohol range'}, {'id': 'C', 'text': 'Usakhelouri and Barakoni wine are tied'}, {'id': 'D', 'text': 'Usakhelouri'}]
source_facts:
[1] Usakhelauri contains 10.5–12.0% alcohol, 3–5% sugar and has 5–7% titrated acidity.
---
[2] Barakoni contains 10-12% alcohol, 1.5-2.5% sugar and has 5-7% titrated acidity.

question_text: Which rootstock, when used in 'Kalecik Karası' vineyards, demonstrated the highest fruit set and Millerandage Index despite elevated boron levels, indicating a pronounced tendency for seedless berry development?
source_facts: [1] Among these, the 5BB rootstock showed the highest fruit set and Millerandage Index, indicating a strong tendency toward the formation of seedless berries despite high boron levels.

question_text: Since 1981, who have been the proprietors of Château de Saurs?
options: [{'id': 'A', 'text': 'The Burrus family of Switzerland'}, {'id': 'B', 'text': 'Marie-Paule Gineste and her husband'}, {'id': 'C', 'text': 'Paul Gineste de Saurs and his wife'}, {'id': 'D', 'text': 'Marie-Paule Burrus and Yves Burrus'}]
source_facts: [1] Since 1981, its proprietors have been Marie-Paule Burrus, a daughter of Paul Gineste de Saurs, and her husband Yves Burrus, a scion of Switzerland's Burrus family of industrialists.
